subject: Gaming
title: Bartle's Player Types
description: Learn Bartle's Player Types in one minute.

hook.text: Not everyone plays games the same way. Richard Bartle identified four types of players in 1996.

buildup.text: Achievers want to complete goals. Explorers want to discover. Socializers want to connect.

discovery.text: Killers want to dominate. Understanding these types helps designers build games for different motivations.

twist.text: Most players are Socializers. But most games are designed for Achievers. There's a massive mismatch.

climax.text: Bartle's taxonomy helped developers understand that one game can't satisfy everyone the same way.

punchline.text: Four types of players. Most games only serve one. A design lesson.

quiz.question: According to Bartle, what are the four player types?
